Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating "by sorry // not yet proven". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.

{"html":"

Signature


functions: check_rep/2[destructor], fst/1, get_rep/1[destructor],
           list/2, pair/2, prog/3, rep/2[private,constructor], report/1, snd/1
equations:
    check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1,
    fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1,
    get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1,
    snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_list:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( list(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( list(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_prog:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ), !KU( x.2 ) ]
  --[ !KU( prog(x, x.1, x.2) ) ]->
   [ !KU( prog(x, x.1, x.2) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_report:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( report(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( report(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_get_rep:
   [ !KD( rep(x.1, x.2) ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

{"html":"

Introduction to Adding Lemmas:

  • Adds the lemma in the current position in the theory, but will throw an error if a lemma with the same name exists, the parsing fails, or the lemma isn't well-formed.
    ‌
  • Adding a lemma will NOT modify the loaded source file, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu appends all added lemmas as a comment at the end of the current theory file.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the added lemmas).

","title":"Add new Lemma"}

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

","title":"Edit Lemma: can_run_v"}

    ‌
  • Deleting a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (so without the deleted lemmas).
    ‌
  • Deleting a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Deleting a source lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


Counter(id,<)


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) Create:
   [ Fr( ~s ) ] --[ Start( ~s ) ]-> [ Counter( ~s, %1 ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Inc:
   [ In( %x ), Counter( s, %x ) ]
  --[ Number( %x ) ]->
   [ Counter( s, (%x%+%1) ) ]

  // loop breaker: [1]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) AttackerMsg:
   [ In( x ) ] --[ Value( x ) ]-> [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) AttackerPub:
   [ In( $x ) ] --[ PubValue( $x ) ]-> [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) AttackerNat:
   [ In( x ), Counter( ~s, x ) ] --[ NatValue( x ) ]-> [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) InOut:
   [ In( %x ), In( (%x%+%1%+%1%+%1) ) ]
  --[ Out( <(%x%+%1%+%1), %1, %1, %x, (%x%+%1), (%x%+%1)> )
  ]->
   [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


","title":"Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions"}

    ‌

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

equations: fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1, snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_h:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( h(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( h(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_lh:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( lh(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( lh(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_rh:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( rh(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( rh(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_rot:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( rot(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( rot(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_xor:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( y ) ] --[ !KU( (x⊕y) ) ]-> [ !KU( (x⊕y) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_zero:
   [ ] --[ !KU( zero ) ]-> [ !KU( zero ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_xor:
   [ !KD( (x⊕y) ), !KU( (y⊕z) ) ] --> [ !KD( (x⊕z) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_xor:
   [ !KD( (x⊕y) ), !KU( y ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating \"by sorry // not yet proven\". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.
?Display this help message.

{"html":"

Signature


builtins: xor
functions: fst/1, h/1, pair/2, snd/1
equations: fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1, snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_h:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( h(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( h(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_xor:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( y ) ] --[ !KU( (x⊕y) ) ]-> [ !KU( (x⊕y) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_zero:
   [ ] --[ !KU( zero ) ]-> [ !KU( zero ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_xor:
   [ !KD( (x⊕y) ), !KU( (y⊕z) ) ] --> [ !KD( (x⊕z) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_xor:
   [ !KD( (x⊕y) ), !KU( y ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating "by sorry // not yet proven". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.
?Display this help message.

{"html":"

Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


K(id)


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) test:

functions: fst/1, pair/2, snd/1
equations: fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1, snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

  • Deleting a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (so without the deleted lemmas).

    ‌
  • Deleting a source lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating \"by sorry // not yet proven\". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.

Signature


functions: fst/1, pair/2, pk/1, sign/2, snd/1, true/0, verify/3

Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:

rule (modulo AC) c_pk:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( pk(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( pk(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_sign:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( sign(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( sign(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_true:
   [ ] --[ !KU( true ) ]-> [ !KU( true ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_verify:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ), !KU( x.2 ) ]
  --[ !KU( verify(x, x.1, x.2) ) ]->
   [ !KU( verify(x, x.1, x.2) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


Counter(id,<)


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) Create:
   [ Fr( ~s ) ] --[ Start( ~s ) ]-> [ Counter( ~s, '1' ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Inc:
   [ Counter( ~s, x ), In( x ) ]
  --[ Counter( ~s, x ) ]->
   [ Counter( ~s, ('1'++x) ), Out( senc(x, ~s) ) ]

  // loop breaker: [0]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


","title":"Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions"}

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating \"by sorry // not yet proven\". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.

{"html":"

Signature


builtins: multiset
functions: fst/1, h/1, pair/2, sdec/2, senc/2, snd/1
equations:
    fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1,
    sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1,
    snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_h:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( h(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( h(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_sdec:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( sdec(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( sdec(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_senc:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( senc(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( senc(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_union:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( y ) ] --[ !KU( (x++y) ) ]-> [ !KU( (x++y) ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_sdec:
   [ !KD( senc(x.1, x.2) ), !KU( x.2 ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_union:
   [ !KD( (x++y) ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


Device_Alice(id)


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) GenKey:
   [ Fr( ~sk ) ]
  --[ Device_Key( ~sk ) ]->
   [ Device_Init( ~sk ), Device_Alice( ~sk ), Out( pk(~sk) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Alice:
   [ Device_Alice( sk ), Fr( ~x ), Fr( ~y ) ]
  --[ Exclusive( ~x, ~y ), Device_Use( sk ) ]->
   [ Device_Alice( sk ), Out( aenc(<~x, ~y>, pk(sk)) ) ]

  // loop breaker: [0]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) DeviceToBob:
   [ Device_Init( sk ), Device_Alice( sk ) ]
  --[ ToBob( sk ) ]->
   [ Device_Select( sk ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Select_Left:
   [ Device_Select( sk ) ] --> [ !Device_Left( sk ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Select_Right:
   [ Device_Select( sk ) ] --> [ !Device_Right( sk ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Decrypt_Left:
   [ !Device_Left( sk ), In( aenc(<x, y>, pk(sk)) ) ]
  --[ Access( x ) ]->
   [ Out( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Decrypt_Right:
   [ !Device_Right( sk ), In( aenc(<x, y>, pk(sk)) ) ]
  --[ Access( y ) ]->
   [ Out( y ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


","title":"Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions"}

{"html":"

Signature


functions: adec/2, aenc/2, fst/1, pair/2, pk/1, snd/1
equations:
    adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1,
    fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1,

Construction Rules

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.

  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating \"by sorry // not yet proven\". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.
?Display this help message.

Signature

    fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1,
    snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2,
    verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_S:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( S(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( S(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_h:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( h(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( h(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pk:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( pk(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( pk(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_sign:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( sign(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( sign(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_true:
   [ ] --[ !KU( true ) ]-> [ !KU( true ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_verify:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ), !KU( x.2 ) ]
  --[ !KU( verify(x, x.1, x.2) ) ]->
   [ !KU( verify(x, x.1, x.2) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


None


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) Register_pk_Bank:
   [ Fr( ~ltkB ) ]
  -->
   [
   !Bank_Ltk( $B, ~ltkB ), !Bank_Pk( $B, pk(~ltkB) ), Out( pk(~ltkB) )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) B_1:
   [ In( blind(~x, r) ), !Bank_Ltk( $B, ~ltkB ) ]
  --[ Withdraw( ~x ) ]->
   [ Out( sign(blind(~x, r), ~ltkB) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) S_1:
   [ In( <~x, sign(~x, ~ltkB)> ), !Bank_Pk( $B, pk(~ltkB) ) ]
  -->
   [ Out( <~x, sign(~x, ~ltkB)> ), St_S_1( $B, ~x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) B_2:
   [ In( <x, sign(x, ~ltkB)> ), !Bank_Ltk( $B, ~ltkB ) ]
  --[ Deposited( x ) ]->
   [ Private_Ch( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) S_2:
   [ Private_Ch( x ), St_S_1( $B, x ) ] --[ Spend( x ) ]-> [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


Restrictions of the Set of Traces


restriction BankOnlyAcceptsOnce:
  "∀ x #j #k.
         ((Deposited( x ) @ #j) ∧ (Deposited( x ) @ #k)) ⇒ (#j = #k)"
  // safety formula

  /*
  expanded formula:
  "∀ x #j #k.
         ((Deposited( x ) @ #j) ∧ (Deposited( x ) @ #k)) ⇒ (#j = #k)"
  */


","title":"Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions"}

Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) Register_pk:
   [ Fr( ~ltkA ) ]
  -->
   [ !Ltk( $A, ~ltkA ), !Pk( $A, pk(~ltkA) ), Out( pk(~ltkA) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Reveal_ltk:
   [ !Ltk( A, ltkA ) ] --[ RevLtk( A ) ]-> [ Out( ltkA ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) I_1:
   [ Fr( ~ni ), !Pk( $R, pkR ) ]
  --[ OUT_I_1( aenc(<'1', ~ni, $I>, pkR) ) ]->
   [
   Out( aenc(<'1', ~ni, $I>, pkR) ),
   St_I_1( $I, $R, ~ni )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) R_1:
   [
   !Ltk( $R, ltkR ), In( aenc(<'1', ni, I>, pk(ltkR)) ),
   !Pk( I, pkI ), Fr( ~nr )
   ]
  --[
  IN_R_1_ni( ni, aenc(<'1', ni, I>, pk(ltkR)) ),
  OUT_R_1( aenc(<'2', ni, ~nr>, pkI) ),
  Running( I, $R, <'init', ni, ~nr> )
  ]->
   [
   Out( aenc(<'2', ni, ~nr>, pkI) ),
   St_R_1( $R, I, ni, ~nr )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) I_2:
   [
   St_I_1( I, R, ni ), !Ltk( I, ltkI ),
   In( aenc(<'2', ni, nr>, pk(ltkI)) ), !Pk( R, pkR )
   ]
  --[
  IN_I_2_nr( nr, aenc(<'2', ni, nr>, pk(ltkI)) ),
  Commit( I, R, <'init', ni, nr> ),
  Running( R, I, <'resp', ni, nr> )
  ]->
   [
   Out( aenc(<'3', nr>, pkR) ), Secret( I, R, nr ),
   Secret( I, R, ni )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) R_2:
   [
   St_R_1( R, I, ni, nr ), !Ltk( R, ltkR ),
   In( aenc(<'3', nr>, pk(ltkR)) )
   ]
  --[ Commit( R, I, <'resp', ni, nr> ) ]->
   [ Secret( R, I, nr ), Secret( R, I, ni ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Secrecy_claim:
   [ Secret( A, B, m ) ] --[ Secret( A, B, m ) ]-> [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


","title":"Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions"}

b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

  • Deleting a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (so without the deleted lemmas).
    ‌
  • Deleting a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Deleting a source lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

    ‌
  • Adding a lemma will NOT modify the loaded source file, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu appends all added lemmas as a comment at the end of the current theory file.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the added lemmas).

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating \"by sorry // not yet proven\". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.
?Display this help message.

  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


Server(id,=,.), Y_counter(id,<)


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) BuyANewYubikey:
   [ Fr( ~k ), Fr( ~pid ), Fr( ~sid ) ]
  --[ Protocol( ), Init( ~pid, ~k ), ExtendedInit( ~pid, ~sid, ~k )
  ]->
   [
   !Y( ~pid, ~sid ), Y_counter( ~pid, '1' ),
   Server( ~pid, ~sid, '1' ), !SharedKey( ~pid, ~k ),
   Out( ~pid )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Yubikey_Plugin:
   [ Y_counter( pid, sc ), In( sc ) ]
  --[ Yubi( pid, ('1'++sc) ) ]->
   [ Y_counter( pid, ('1'++sc) ) ]

  // loop breaker: [0]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Yubikey_PressButton:
   [
   !Y( pid, sid ), Y_counter( pid, tc ), !SharedKey( pid, k ),
   In( tc ), Fr( ~npr ), Fr( ~nonce )
   ]
  --[
  YubiPress( pid, tc ),
  YubiOTP( pid, senc(<sid, tc, ~npr>, k) ),
  YubiSid( pid, sid, k )
  ]->
   [
   Y_counter( pid, ('1'++tc) ),
   Out( <pid, ~nonce, senc(<sid, tc, ~npr>, k)> )
   ]

  // loop breaker: [1]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Server_ReceiveOTP_NewSession:
   [
   Server( pid, sid, otc ),
   In( <pid, nonce, senc(<sid, tc, ~pr>, k)> ),
   !SharedKey( pid, k ), In( otc )
   ]
  --[
  Login( pid, sid, tc, senc(<sid, tc, ~pr>, k) ),
  LoginCounter( pid, otc, tc ), Smaller( otc, tc )
  ]->
   [ Server( pid, sid, tc ) ]

  // loop breaker: [0]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


Restrictions of the Set of Traces


restriction smaller:
  "∀ #i a b. (Smaller( a, b ) @ #i) ⇒ (∃ z. (a++z) = b)"

  /*
  expanded formula:
  "∀ #i a b. (Smaller( a, b ) @ #i) ⇒ (∃ z. (a++z) = b)"
  */

Theory: YubikeyMultisets (Loaded at 23:57:26 from Local "/tmp/tmp.KywXiwLxlZ/thy/Yubikey_multiset.spthy")

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating \"by sorry // not yet proven\". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.
?Display this help message.

","title":"Theory: YubikeyMultisets"}

{"html":"

Signature


builtins: multiset
functions: fst/1, pair/2, sdec/2, senc/2, snd/1
equations:
    fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1,
    sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1,
    snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2


Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_sdec:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( sdec(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( sdec(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_senc:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( senc(x, x.1) ) ]->
   [ !KU( senc(x, x.1) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( snd(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( snd(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) coerce:
   [ !KD( x ) ] --[ !KU( x ) ]-> [ !KU( x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) pub:
   [ ] --[ !KU( $x ) ]-> [ !KU( $x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_union:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( y ) ] --[ !KU( (x++y) ) ]-> [ !KU( (x++y) ) ]


Deconstruction Rules


rule (modulo AC) d_0_fst:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_sdec:
   [ !KD( senc(x.1, x.2) ), !KU( x.2 ) ] --> [ !KD( x.1 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_0_snd:
   [ !KD( <x.1, x.2> ) ] --> [ !KD( x.2 ) ]

rule (modulo AC) d_union:
   [ !KD( (x++y) ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

Quick introduction

Left pane: Proof scripts display.

  • When a theory is initially loaded, there will be a line at the end of each theorem stating "by sorry // not yet proven". Click on sorry to inspect the proof state.
  • Right-click to show further options, such as autoprove.

Right pane: Visualization.

  • Visualization and information display relating to the currently selected item.

Keyboard shortcuts

j/kJump to the next/previous proof path within the currently focused lemma.
J/KJump to the next/previous open constraint within the currently focused lemma, or to the next/previous lemma if there are no more sorry steps in the proof of the current lemma.
1-9Apply the proof method with the given number as shown in the applicable proof method section in the main view.
a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.

Construction Rules


rule (modulo AC) c_fst:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ !KU( fst(x) ) ]-> [ !KU( fst(x) ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_null:

rule (modulo AC) c_pair:
   [ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ]
  --[ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]->
   [ !KU( <x, x.1> ) ]

rule (modulo AC) c_prog:

rule (modulo AC) c_snd:

rule (modulo AC) c_succ:

rule (modulo AC) coerce:

rule (modulo AC) pub:

rule (modulo AC) fresh:
   [ Fr( ~x ) ] --[ !KU( ~x ) ]-> [ !KU( ~x ) ]


Deconstruction Rules

rule (modulo AC) d_0_get_rep:

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.

  • Adds the lemma in the current position in the theory, but will throw an error if a lemma with the same name exists, the parsing fails, or the lemma isn't well-formed.
    ‌
  • Adding a lemma will NOT modify the loaded source file, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu appends all added lemmas as a comment at the end of the current theory file.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the added lemmas).

","title":"Add new Lemma"}

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

   [ Fr( ~ni1 ), Fr( ~rnd1 ), Fr( ~ltkV1 ) ]
  -->
   [
   Out( commit(~ni1, ~rnd1, pk(~ltkV1)) ),
   Out( fake(~ni1, ~rnd1, ~ltkV1, ~ni1) ), Out( ~ltkV1 )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


","title":"Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions"}

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

rule (modulo E) Register_Corrupted_Bank_pk:
   [ Fr( ~ltkB ) ]
  --[ OnlyOnce( ) ]->
   [ !Bank_Ltk( $B, ~ltkB ), !Bank_Pk( $B, pk(~ltkB) ), Out( ~ltkB ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) regular_agent:
   [ Fr( ~C ) ] --> [ !Customer( ~C ), Out( ~C ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) test_subject:
   [ Fr( ~C ) ] --[ TestSubject( ~C ) ]-> [ !Customer( ~C ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) C_1:
   [ Fr( ~x ), Fr( ~r ), !Customer( ~C ) ]
  --[ Mint( (~C⊕~x), ~x, ~r ), Mint( ~x, ~x, ~r ) ]->
   [
   Out( <blind((~C⊕~x), ~r), blind(~x, ~r)> ),
   !St_C_1( ~C, ~x, ~r )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) C_2:
   [
   !St_C_1( C, ~y, ~r ), In( sign(blind(x, ~r), ~skB) ),
   !Bank_Pk( $B, pk(~skB) )
   ]
  --[ Coin( x, ~y, ~r ), Spend( C, ~y, ~r ) ]->
   [ Out( <x, sign(x, ~skB)> ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

a/AApply the autoprove method to the focused proof step. a stops after finding a solution, and A searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
b/BApply a bounded-depth version of the autoprove method to the focused proof step. b stops after finding a solution, and B searches for all solutions. Needs to have a sorry selected to work.
s/SApply the autoprove method to all lemmas. s stops after finding a solution, and S searches for all solutions.

{"html":"

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.

Introduction to Lemma Edit:

  • Modifying the lemma in the box above and clicking the submit button will attempt to modify the lemma in the current theory.
    ‌
  • Failures in parsing the lemma or verifying its well-formedness will result in an error, and the lemma will NOT be modified. However, your changes will be kept on this page until you leave this right panel.
    ‌
  • Editing a lemma will NOT modify the file it was loaded from, but clicking on "Append modified lemmas to file" in the Actions menu adds all modified lemmas as a comment at the end of the file on disk they were loaded from.
    ‌
  • Clicking on "Download source" in the Actions menu will download the modified version of the theory (including the modified lemmas), but not modify the file on disk.
    ‌
  • Modifying a reuse lemma will invalidate all subsequent proofs.
    ‌
  • Modifying a sources lemma is not supported and will result in an error.
